zh.Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Engla: 英國英格蘭越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Vavasour於大約1400年出生在英國越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特時，她的父親Henry Vavasour III22歲，母親Margaret Skipwith II17歲。她於1426年7月之前與Hamon Sutton I結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1455年在英國林可爾史爾林肯享年56歲。

zh-hans.Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Engla: 英国英格兰越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特

nameMeanings.name: Vavasour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: status name for a feudal tenant ranking immediately below a baron, Old French vavas(s)our, vavasseur, Latin vassus vassorum ‘vassal of vassals’.

variants.name: Bavister
meanings: English: Titford suggests that this is a late variant of Vavasour . It would be a dissimilated form of Vavister and Vavester, variants of Vavasour that are evidenced in Lincs, also in Cambs, whence the name spread into Beds. Compare Willa Vavister, 1579 in IGI (Scopwick, Lincs); Thomas Vavister, 1615 in IGI (Cranwell, Lincs); Mary Vavester, 1708, Joseph Vavisor, 1738 in IGI (New Sleaford, Lincs).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Vavasour was born about 1400, in Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, England, her father, Henry Vavasour III, was 22 and her mother, Margaret Skipwith II, was 17. She married Hamon Sutton I before July 1426. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1455, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 56.

Elle a épousé Hamon Sutton I avant juillet 1426. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1455, à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.
